title: Communication in Dev Teams
summary: Clear communication is as important as coding on a team.
It means asking good questions, sharing progress, and writing clearly.
Much of it happens in writing, like chat and tickets.
It is often what separates good developers from great ones.

sections.text: It is tempting to think development is all about code, but on a team, how you communicate matters just as much. Sharing what you are doing, asking for help well, and writing clearly are skills that shape how effective you really are.

sections.title: Why Communication Matters
sections.text: Software is built by groups, so the work depends on people understanding each other. A brilliant solution nobody knows about, or a blocker you stayed silent on, helps no one. Communicating clearly, what you are working on, what is blocking you, what you decided, keeps the whole team moving together.

sections.title: Mostly in Writing
sections.text: A lot of team communication is written, in chat, tickets, code review comments, and documentation, especially on remote teams across time zones. Asking a clear question, giving a useful update, and explaining a decision in writing are everyday skills. Doing them well builds trust and saves everyone time.

media.alt: Clear written updates and questions flowing between remote teammates

sections.title: Players Calling the Ball
sections.text: Good team communication is like players on a field calling for the ball. The most skilled players still constantly say what they are doing and what they need. Silence leads to collisions and missed passes. A team that talks, even briefly and clearly, plays far better than a group of talented individuals who do not.

media.alt: Teammates calling for the ball to avoid collisions and missed passes

sections.title: Communicating Well on a Team
items: Share what you are working on.
Speak up early when you are blocked.
Write updates and decisions down clearly.
Keep it brief and considerate of others' time.

sections.text: Many experienced developers say communication, not raw coding skill, is what most often separates a good developer from a great one, especially as you grow into more senior roles.

terms.definition: Telling others what you are working on.
Something stopping your progress to raise early.
What clear communication builds over time.

questions.text: How important is communication on a team?
questions.explanation: On a team, clear communication is as important as coding.

options: Asking good questions, sharing progress, and writing clearly
questions.explanation: It includes asking good questions, sharing progress, and writing clearly.

questions.text: Where does much of it happen?
options: In writing, like chat and tickets
questions.explanation: Much team communication happens in writing, like chat and tickets.
